summary: Iter-28 (frontend-only): validated the AI-prescription → player-coherence fix on /app/frontend/src/lib/audioEngine.js + /app/frontend/src/components/Dashboard.jsx. Wrote a single Playwright run that exercised the full user-reported flow against the live preview (https://frequency-healer-31.preview.emergentagent.com) as admin@example.com (Pro). 20/22 sub-assertions PASSED — the spec'd fix DOES deliver: (1) AI Generate auto-starts the engine and flips the play/pause icon to Pause (T1 ✅), (2) clicking the player STOPS everything and master ramps to silence within ~1s (T2 ✅, amplitude=0), (3) the prescription's freq/waveform/binaural/isochronic survive a stop→play resume cycle (T3 ✅), (4) setAmbient() while STOPPED no longer produces audio and stores into _pendingAmbient (T4 ✅), (5) setAmbient() while PLAYING still live-ramps as before (T6 ✅, ocean ramped 0→0.497 in 1.2s — no regression), (6) running a NEW AI prescription mid-session hot-swaps without ever flipping playing=false (T7 ✅, 46/46 samples true across the call). HOWEVER, a critical regression was caught by T5: when the engine is stopped, setAmbient is recorded into _pendingAmbient correctly, but clicking Play does NOT activate the recorded mix — the ambient gain stays at 0 even though the slider UI shows 50% and ambient.volume=0.5. Reproducible 100% via the exact pause→resume path the spec calls out as the resume mechanism. RCA: ordering bug in audioEngine.start() — the _pendingAmbient restore loop runs BEFORE `this.playing = true`, so each restored entry re-enters the !this.playing branch of setAmbient, which silently re-records it into _pendingAmbient and forces gain to 0; start() then nulls _pendingAmbient, so the entire stored mix is dropped on the floor. This is precisely the user-reported production bug: 'when the AI Prescription makes a selection for the user and starts playing, ambient sounds … could not be stopped or started back from the player' — the player APPEARS to play but the prescribed ambient layers are silent on resume.

integration_issues.flow: Resume after Stop (AI prescription pause→play, OR setAmbient-while-stopped → Play)
integration_issues.issue: audioEngine.start() restores _pendingAmbient BEFORE setting this.playing=true, so the restore-loop's setAmbient() calls hit the !this.playing branch, get re-routed into _pendingAmbient, and the very next line (this._pendingAmbient=null) drops them. Net effect: ambient gain stays at 0 after the resume even though ambient.volume reads 0.5 and the React slider UI shows 50%. The user hears the tone but no rain/ocean/etc. EXACT reproduction of the production complaint.

action_items: FIX audioEngine.start() ordering: set `this.playing = true` BEFORE the `_pendingAmbient` restore loop (around line 346 of /app/frontend/src/lib/audioEngine.js). Suggested minimal patch — move the `this.playing = true` assignment (currently line 353) up to just before the `if (this._pendingAmbient)` block. setAmbient() will then take the playing-branch and actually ramp the gain up. After the fix, re-run iter-28 T5 (setAmbient-while-stopped → Play activates pending rain) and T3 with an AI prescription that has a non-empty ambient mix (e.g., 'I want rain and gentle focus') to confirm the ambient mix survives a stop/start cycle.

(Optional) Add a soundscape-stopped E2E to the next iter: the soundscapes-section is rendered below the fold and needs `page.locator('[data-testid="soundscape-rain"]').scroll_into_view_if_needed()` before click. T8 was inconclusive in this run because the soundscape button was not in the rendered DOM at the moment of query (likely lazy-mounted on scroll, or just outside the 1080-tall viewport). The underlying gate logic IS verified by T4 since soundscape-button → audioEngine.setAmbient(kind, vol) is the same code path.
critical_code_review_comments: audioEngine.js:346-353 — The fix correctly gated setAmbient on this.playing, but it broke its own restore mechanism. The start() sequence should be: (1) build/start oscillators, (2) set this.playing=true, (3) restore _pendingAmbient. Currently it's (1) → (3) → (2). With the gate now in setAmbient(), step 3 MUST come after step 2.

Dashboard.jsx:555-557 — Calling audioEngine.start() at the tail of runAiRecommend() is correct, and the user-gesture chain is preserved (the Generate click → await fetch → engine.start() all stay within the same gesture-promise lineage thanks to the new AudioContext unlock-on-construction model). UI ↔ engine state coherence verified.

audioEngine.js:717-728 — The new 'stopped' branch of setAmbient is correctly written: it caps the actual GainNode to 0 (using cancelScheduledValues + setValueAtTime), and records the desired volume in _pendingAmbient. Defensive — handles the v=0 case by deleting the key. Pre-built BufferSource noise nodes remain running but inaudible — correct.

audioEngine.js:710 — `a.volume = v` is set BEFORE the playing check, meaning ambient.volume gets out-of-sync with the actual GainNode while stopped (volume=0.5 but gain=0). For the slider UI binding this is fine (it tracks .volume), but it means stop()'s snapshot at line 484-486 (`if (a.volume > 0.001) snap[k] = a.volume`) would ALSO snapshot stopped-only mixes. Not currently a bug because stop() short-circuits when !this.playing, but worth a comment to prevent future regressions.
Carry-over from iter-27: /app/backend/server.py is at 1898 lines — still well past the 700-line guideline; recommend a routers/ extraction in a future iter (not in scope of this bug).

success_rate.frontend: 91% (20/22 sub-assertions; the 1 functional failure (T5) is the critical regression; T8 was inconclusive due to off-screen soundscape buttons)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run the same Playwright flow used in iter-28 after the main agent patches audioEngine.start() to set this.playing=true BEFORE the _pendingAmbient restore loop. The critical assertion to watch is T5 (setAmbient(rain,0.5) while stopped → click Play → ambient.rain.gain.gain.value should be > 0.4 within ~1.5s). Also run T3 with an AI intent that produces a non-empty ambient mix (e.g. 'rainy day deep focus' or 'forest meditation'); the prescription returned during iter-28 ('help me focus' → 40Hz isochronic) had an empty ambient mix and so T3 trivially passed despite the underlying bug. The other 19 PASS assertions should continue to pass — the fix is a one-line reorder. The soundscapes-section is rendered but lives below the 1080 fold; scroll-into-view before click, or query window.__audioEngine.setAmbient directly (which uses the same gate code path). Engine handles are exposed as window.__audioEngine; key inspection fields: .playing, ._pendingAmbient, .ambient[kind].gain.gain.value, .ambient[kind].volume, .getAmplitude(), .frequency, .waveform, .binaural, .isochronic.
rca of the issue: PRODUCTION BUG ROOT CAUSE: audioEngine.start() restores _pendingAmbient before setting this.playing=true. With the new setAmbient gate added in this iter, the restore-loop's setAmbient calls hit the !this.playing branch, which routes the volumes back into _pendingAmbient and forces gain to 0; start() then nulls _pendingAmbient. RESULT: ambient mix from any AI prescription is silently dropped on resume; user hears the tone alone while the React slider UI still shows 50%. REPRODUCTION: login as admin → click play to unlock → click play to stop → in DevTools console, audioEngine._pendingAmbient=null; audioEngine.setAmbient('rain',0.5) → audioEngine.start() → audioEngine.ambient.rain.gain.gain.value === 0 (BUG; should be ~0.5). MITIGATION: move `this.playing = true` (currently line 353 of audioEngine.js) up to BEFORE the `if (this._pendingAmbient)` block at line 346. One-line fix, no other changes needed.
